summary: Russia launched a combined ballistic missile and drone attack on Ukraine, with an Iskander-M ballistic missile striking Kyiv's Darnytskyi district and gas stations targeted in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia regions, causing civilian casualties. Ukrainian forces reported downing drones over the capital and conducted strikes on oil infrastructure in Krasnodar Krai, while the 39th Tactical Aviation Brigade was awarded the honorary name of Hero of Ukraine Oleksandr Dovhach. The EU confirmed Ukraine will receive a €3.7 billion tranche in September as part of €8.5 billion in budget support this year, with two-thirds directed to military needs.
key_points: An Iskander-M ballistic missile struck Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, causing a fire at a warehouse and debris falling on open territory, with four people injured at a gas station in Sumy region and one woman wounded in Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162, @UkraineNow/82181)
Russia destroyed more than 150 gas stations in Ukraine over a two-month period, according to former Infrastructure Minister Pivovarsky. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31168)

Ukrainian drones struck an oil depot in the Krasnodar Region (Krasnoarmeysky district, Poltava stanitsa), causing a fire and temporarily blocking highway traffic toward Trudobelikovsky farm. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Ukraine will receive a second tranche of €3.7 billion from the EU in September, with two-thirds of the €8.5 billion annual budget support directed toward direct military needs. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31158)
The 39th Tactical Aviation Brigade of the Ukrainian Air Force was awarded the honorary name of Hero of Ukraine Oleksandr Dovhach via Presidential Decree No. 527/2026. (Source: @kpszsu/66368)

The commander of the 'Skelia' assault regiment, Yuriy Harkavy, was suspended from duties during a State Bureau of Investigation inquiry following a Babel publication about torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers in the unit. (Source: @UkraineNow/82178)

Ukrainian assault units of the 'North' airborne division liberated the village of Ivolzhanskoe on the Sumy front and continue offensive actions near Kachiy Lopan, Volchansk, Velikoburlyuk, and Kupyansk sectors. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Belarusian President Lukashenko claimed a Ukrainian drone strike hit a bus with Belarusian children in Bryansk region, calling it a provocation, while also stating he recently communicated with Zelensky's representatives. (Source: @tass_agency/382391, @UkraineNow/82176)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a combined Iskander-M ballistic missile and drone attack across multiple Ukrainian regions, with strikes on Kyiv, Sumy, and Zaporizhzhia causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukraine's 39th Tactical Aviation Brigade received a presidential honorary designation, and the 'Skelia' regiment commander was suspended amid an SBI investigation into alleged torture of mobilized soldiers. The EU confirmed a €3.7 billion disbursement to Ukraine in September, with the majority earmarked for military needs.

points: The 39th Tactical Aviation Brigade of the Armed Forces of Ukraine was awarded the honorary name of Hero of Ukraine Oleksandr Dovhach via Presidential Decree No. 527/2026, recognizing the legacy of the legendary pilot and commander. (Source: @kpszsu/66368)

The commander of the 'Skelia' assault regiment, Yuriy Harkavy, was removed from his duties for the duration of the State Bureau of Investigation's inquiry following a Babel media report documenting torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers within the unit. An acting commander has not yet been appointed. (Source: @UkraineNow/82178)

Ukraine will receive a second tranche of €3.7 billion from the European Union in September, according to EC spokesman Balázs Ujváry, out of €8.5 billion in total budget support for the year, with two-thirds allocated to direct military needs. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31158)

The first tranche of the €90 billion EU aid package no longer includes €5.9 billion for drone production; instead the EU will purchase drones directly to avoid corruption schemes involving the Ukrainian side, with €3.2 billion provided as direct budget support. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31148)

Ukrainian assault units of the 'North' airborne division drove out enemy forces from the village of Ivolzhanskoe on the Sumy front and continue offensive operations in Kachiy Lopan, the Volchansk sector (Losevka, Ukrainka, Zemlyany Yar), Velikoburlyuk sector (Petro-Ivanovka), and east of Oskol on the Kupyansk front. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Multiple UAVs were detected heading toward Kyiv from northern and northeastern directions, with activity also reported along the Chernihiv-Sumy border region moving southward, toward Sumy from the north, toward Zaporizhzhia from the south, and toward Kharkiv from the northeast. (Source: @kpszsu/66393, @kpszsu/66363, @kpszsu/66397, @kpszsu/66389)

Ukrainian drones struck an oil depot in Krasnodar Krai at the Poltava stanitsa in the Krasnoarmeysky district, causing a fire and temporarily blocking highway traffic toward Trudobelikovsky farm. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Russia launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile that struck Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, causing a fire at a warehouse facility and debris falling on open territory. (Source: @UkraineNow/82181, @mon1tor_ua/68046)
A nighttime Russian attack involved an Iskander-M ballistic missile along with 90 drones targeting multiple regions across Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

Russian forces struck gas stations in Sumy region (injuring two gas station workers) and Zaporizhzhia (wounding one woman), with visible smoke reported in Zaporizhzhia after an attack on a gas station. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)

Five people were injured in the city of Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv region from a Russian strike that damaged a building facade, power line, and destroyed a garage and three vehicles. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
Russian forces destroyed more than 150 gas stations in Ukraine over a two-month period, according to former Ukrainian Infrastructure Minister Pivovarsky. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31168)

Russian tactical aviation was reported active on the southeastern direction with a threat of air-launched strike weapons for frontline regions, and also on the northeastern and eastern directions. (Source: @kpszsu/66392, @kpszsu/66364)

Russian forces conducted individual strikes on Sumy, Zaporozhye, Dnepropetrovsk, and Poltava regions, including hitting a gas distribution substation in Zaporozhye region and an industrial facility in Poltava region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

On the Kharkiv front, GRU 'North' assault units continue offensive actions in Kachiy Lopan and adjacent forested areas, as well as in the Volchansk sector villages of Losevka, Ukrainka, and Zemlyany Yar. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
On the Velikoburlyuk sector of the Kharkiv front, Ukrainian assault groups are fighting in Petro-Ivanovka and surrounding areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
On the Kupyansk front, battles continue east of Oskol. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Five civilians were injured in Bohodukhiv, Kharkiv region, from a Russian strike damaging a building facade, power line, garage, and three vehicles. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
UAVs were detected moving toward Kharkiv from the northeastern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66389)
Russian tactical aviation was reported active on the eastern direction with a threat of air-launched strike weapons for frontline regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66364)
On the Sumy front, the 'North' airborne division's assault aircraft drove out the last enemy groups from the village of Ivolzhanskoe and liberated the settlement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
In the Shostka district of Sumy region, skirmishes were reported in and around Bachevsk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
Four people were injured in Sumy region from a Russian attack, including two gas station workers hospitalized with minor injuries. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
UAVs were detected moving toward Sumy from the north and along the Chernihiv-Sumy border in a southern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66397, @kpszsu/66363)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was reported for northern areas. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68037)
UAV activity was reported in northern Chernihiv region heading west/southwest, and along the Dnipro district line from Dobryanka toward Slavutich. (Source: @kpszsu/66349, @eRadarrua/98163)

In Zaporizhzhia region, Russian forces hit a gas distribution substation, and a gas station attack in the city of Zaporizhzhia wounded one woman with visible smoke reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151, @UkraineNow/82162)
UAVs were detected moving toward Zaporizhzhia from the south and from eastern Dnipropetrovsk region toward Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @kpszsu/66397, @kpszsu/66395)
Russian tactical aviation was reported active on the southeastern direction with a threat of air-launched strike weapons for frontline regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66392)

Ukrainian drones struck an oil depot in Krasnodar Krai at the Poltava stanitsa in the Krasnoarmeysky district, causing a fire and temporarily blocking the highway toward Trudobelikovsky farm. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Combat with enemy drones was reported in Crimea and Sevastopol, with Zelensky noting the peninsula as a focus of enemy attention for strike drone operations aimed at complicating logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
An Iskander-M ballistic missile struck Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, causing a warehouse fire and debris fallout on open territory. (Source: @UkraineNow/82181, @mon1tor_ua/68046)
A nighttime Russian attack combined an Iskander-M ballistic missile with 90 drones targeting multiple Ukrainian regions. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162)
Several drones flying toward Kyiv were shot down during the night. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Russian tactical aviation was reported active on the northeastern, eastern, and southeastern directions with threats of air-launched strike weapons for frontline regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66364, @kpszsu/66387, @kpszsu/66392)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68037)

The relatively low number of Ukrainian drone strikes and rare missile launches in recent days may indicate a serious buildup of strike weapons, according to the morning report. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)

Multiple UAVs were detected across northern, eastern, and southern directions including toward Kyiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, and Zaporizhzhia from various approach vectors. (Source: @kpszsu/66393, @kpszsu/66389, @kpszsu/66397, @kpszsu/66395)

key_developments: Russia launched a combined Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90-drone attack on Ukraine, with the ballistic missile striking Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, causing a warehouse fire and debris fallout. (Source: @UkraineNow/82162, @UkraineNow/82181)

Russian forces destroyed more than 150 gas stations in Ukraine over a two-month period, with strikes on gas stations in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia causing civilian casualties. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31168, @UkraineNow/82162)

Ukrainian drones struck an oil depot in Krasnodar Krai at the Poltava stanitsa in the Krasnoarmeysky district, causing a fire and temporarily blocking the highway toward Trudobelikovsky farm. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
The 39th Tactical Aviation Brigade was awarded the honorary name of Hero of Ukraine Oleksandr Dovhach via Presidential Decree No. 527/2026. (Source: @kpszsu/66368)
The commander of the 'Skelia' assault regiment was suspended during an SBI investigation following a Babel report on torture and deaths of mobilized soldiers in the unit. (Source: @UkraineNow/82178)

Ukrainian 'North' airborne division liberated Ivolzhanskoe on the Sumy front and continues offensive actions in Kachiy Lopan, Volchansk, Velikoburlyuk, and Kupyansk sectors. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151)
The EU confirmed Ukraine will receive a €3.7 billion tranche in September, with two-thirds of the €8.5 billion annual budget support directed to direct military needs. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31158)
sections.threat_assessment: The combined Iskander-M and 90-drone attack represents a significant escalation in Russia's overnight strike campaign targeting civilian energy infrastructure and frontline regions. The destruction of over 150 gas stations in two months signals a systematic Russian effort to degrade Ukraine's energy distribution network. Continued high Russian tactical aviation activity on the northeastern, eastern, and southeastern directions poses an ongoing threat to frontline regions with air-launched weapons. Ukraine's drone strike on Krasnodar Krai demonstrates continued Ukrainian ability to project strikes into Russian territory targeting logistics infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: The European Union confirmed a €3.7 billion disbursement to Ukraine in September as part of €8.5 billion in annual budget support, with two-thirds earmarked for direct military needs, and shifted to direct EU drone procurement to avoid corruption risks. Moldova published a list of CIS country artists deemed security risks, potentially denying them entry. Trump publicly stated Zelensky is 'doing well' and described him as brave with 'great equipment' and 'great people,' marking a shift from earlier assessments. France reportedly detained a Russian-linked tanker near Sicily that Macron claimed was part of Russia's shadow fleet operating in violation of maritime law.
sections.intelligence_notes: The suspension of the 'Skelia' regiment commander during an SBI investigation into alleged torture of mobilized soldiers represents a potential accountability development within Ukrainian military formations. Lukashenko's claim of recent contact with Zelensky's representatives, juxtaposed with his accusation of a Ukrainian drone strike on a bus with Belarusian children in Bryansk region, suggests ongoing Belarus-Russia-Ukraine diplomatic complexity. The relatively low Ukrainian drone and missile activity noted in recent days may indicate a deliberate buildup of strike weapons, warranting monitoring for a potential escalation in Ukrainian long-range strike operations.
